questions.question: How does a DBMS typically recover from a deadlock?
options: By aborting (rolling back) one of the transactions

questions.question: Which concurrency anomaly occurs when a transaction reads data that has been modified by another transaction that has not yet committed?
What does the Atomicity property ensure?
options: All operations execute or none do

questions.question: What is the Two-Phase Locking (2PL) protocol?
options: A protocol ensuring serializability using Growing and Shrinking phases
A protocol with two locks per table
A protocol that prevents all deadlocks
A protocol that locks two rows at a time

questions.question: What is a Dirty Read?
options: Reading uncommitted data from another transaction
Reading data from a deleted table

questions.question: Which Isolation Level prevents Dirty Reads, Non-repeatable Reads, and Phantoms?
What is a savepoint?
options: A point in a transaction you can rollback to

questions.question: How does Wait-Die scheme handle deadlocks?
What happens during a system crash if a transaction was committed but not written to disk?
options: Durability property ensures it is recovered from the log
